title: TypeScript for large web projects | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)

seo.title: TypeScript for large web projects | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)
seo.description: Why TypeScript is often worth using in larger web projects, internal systems, and products with multiple contributors or complex integrations.

page.title: TypeScript for large web projects | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)
page.h1: TypeScript for larger web projects and long-term product work
page.description: Why TypeScript is often worth using in larger web projects, internal systems, and products with multiple contributors or complex integrations.

hero.title: TypeScript is not strictness for its own sake
hero.subtitle: Its real value appears when systems grow, several people work in the codebase, and data or interface mistakes become expensive.

intro: In larger projects the problem is rarely syntax. It is uncertainty about what flows where and what each part of the system expects.
TypeScript helps create clearer boundaries across the codebase and reduces the class of mistakes that would otherwise appear only at runtime.

sections.title: Where this technology is a strong fit
body: Technology should fit the product and delivery reality, not just developer preference.
bullets: larger teams or longer product life
more complex data and integration boundaries

sections.title: What to be careful about
body: A technology’s strengths disappear quickly when it is used in the wrong context or without a sound architecture.

sections.title: Typical fit for a business project
body: The choice should support delivery speed, maintainability, and operational confidence over time.

faq.question: Is TypeScript necessary for small projects too?
faq.answer: Not always. Its value grows with complexity, collaboration, and long-term maintenance needs.

faq.question: Does TypeScript slow development down?
faq.answer: It can add discipline in the short term, but in larger projects that is often paid back through lower error rates and safer change.

faq.question: Does TypeScript guarantee good architecture?
faq.answer: No. It is a support tool, not a substitute for sound design.
